arguments.task: Open official Gorgias pages needed to verify product description, developer API surface, authentication methods, rate limits, pricing/access, and whether an MCP server exists. Prefer first-party pages on gorgias.com or developers.gorgias.com. Read and return concise findings with exact supporting canonical URLs for: 1) one-line product description, 2) documented auth methods for the API, 3) API type and breadth, 4) rate limit notes, 5) pricing or plan/access language relevant to whether API access is self-serve or gated, and 6) any official MCP/server/Model Context Protocol page if it exists. Do not rely on search result snippets; open the pages and read them.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Gorgias sources.

raw_result.description: Customer support helpdesk platform with AI agents for ecommerce brands.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs describe OAuth 2.0 for public apps and API keys for private apps; personal/API keys are mapped here as token.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence indicates API access/rate entitlements are included on paid plans, with Enterprise explicitly listing API throughput.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with endpoints across tickets, customers, macros, and related helpdesk resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Gorgias MCP or Model Context Protocol server page was found in the gathered official evidence.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears tied to paid plans rather than a clearly documented free self-serve developer tier.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limitations page describes a leaky-bucket model with 80 requests per 20 seconds for OAuth apps and 40 requests per 20 seconds for API keys.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-structured first-party developer portal with dedicated introduction, authentication, and limitations pages; supports both public app OAuth and private app API-key usage.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered evidence supports auth methods, REST API type, and paid-plan gating, but MCP absence is only based on not finding an official page in the gathered evidence rather than an explicit first-party statement of nonexistence.

result.description: Customer support helpdesk platform with AI agents for ecommerce brands.
